q: The wavelength of the laser mentioned in Example 7.5 is $6328 \mathring{A}$.
Stimulated emission is a process where the atom in its excited state interacts with an incident photon.
The optical cavity is also called a resonator.
For stimulated emission to occur, the population of the lower level must be higher than the upper level.
Laser beams are typically divergent and broad over short distances.
The laser medium is the material emitting the laser beam.
Laser light is monochromatic.
Spectrofluorometers are a type of spectrometer.
Total internal reflection allows light to bounce down the length of the fibre cable.
Laser light is never used in optical fibre communications.
A perfect reflector has a reflectivity of almost 100%.
Laser action is sustained when the optical cavity reflects light back and forth.
Endoscopes use optical fibres to observe an image inside the body.
The dispersion element in a spectrometer can be a prism or grating.
Lasers are used in space communication such as radars and satellites.
Refraction and absorption cause the light beam in an optical fibre to attenuate.
The core of an optical fibre is composed of two concentric layers.
In a spectrophotometer, a sample is placed between the monochromator and the detector.
The laser beam is narrow because it is coherent.
Commercial lasers can produce up to a megawatt of power.
Photometry is the science of measuring visible light in units that are weighted according to the sensitivity of the human eye.
The human eye responds to light having wavelengths between about 200 nm and 500 nm.
The human eye is more sensitive to green light of 555 nm compared to other colors.
Luminous flux is a measurement of the power of visible light produced by a light source.
One lumen is equivalent to $1/683$ watt of light with a wavelength of 555 nm.
The unit of luminous intensity is the lux.
The unit of illuminance is the candela.
A solid angle is expressed in steradian.
The solid angle subtended at the centre by the whole surface area of the sphere is $2\pi$ steradian.
Luminance is the luminous intensity per unit area of light travelling in a given direction.
The SI unit for luminance is lux.
The minimum average illuminance for school study halls should be 500 lux.
Illuminance is directly proportional to the square of the distance.
Farsightedness is a defect of vision in which distant objects are seen clearly, but objects nearby are blurry.
Nearsightedness is a defect of vision in which near objects are seen clearly, but objects far away are blurry.
A laser produces a wide beam of scattered, incoherent light.
A microscope uses lenses to make enlarged images of objects that are too small for the unaided eye to see.
The optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a telescope.
Luminous intensity is defined as luminous flux emitted by a light source in a direction per unit solid angle.
The wavelength range of human eye sensitivity is between 400 nm and 700 nm.
